text.title: rust-ownership Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for rust-ownership as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Adding .clone() every time the borrow checker complains without investigating why.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: clone() allocates and copies; it is a band-aid over a design flaw in the ownership graph. Budget rule: if the same function signature has been changed 3+ times for borrow errors, simplify the design (return owned, restructure the data).

failure-mode.description: Returning a reference to a local value (dangling reference).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The local is dropped at function exit; the reference points to freed memory. Compiler rejects it, but LLMs sometimes suggest workarounds like Box::leak — avoid.

failure-mode.description: Using fn foo(s: &String) instead of fn foo(s: &str).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Clippy lint ptr_arg catches this. &String forces callers to own a String; &str accepts any string slice.

failure-mode.description: Adding 'static to every lifetime parameter to silence "does not outlive 'static" errors.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Poisons the API — forces callers to own or leak data. Walk back to the original mismatch; usually data is borrowed when it should be owned.
